notes: These files are designed for rendering detailed type information, not just range checking.
Fields are intentionally verbose so the UI can choose what to show.
mainstreamSizes describes common ABIs/platforms; standardGuarantee describes what the language standard itself promises.
languageNotes: Rust fixed-width integers are explicit. usize/isize are pointer-sized and at least 16 bits.
Rust debug and release overflow behavior differs for built-in integer operators unless you use explicit wrapping/checked/saturating APIs.

mainstreamSizes.platform: all Rust targets that support the type

specialRules: In debug builds, overflow in ordinary arithmetic checks and panics by default; in optimized release-style builds it wraps in two's-complement style unless explicit overflow checks are enabled.
Use wrapping_add, checked_add, saturating_add, overflowing_add when semantics must be explicit.

types.definition: Signed integer with the same number of bits as the platform pointer type.
types.summary: Useful for pointer differences, indexing-related signed math, and machine-word-sized arithmetic.
types.standardGuarantee: At least 16 bits wide; same width as pointer-sized signed integer on the target.
specialRules: Do not assume 32 or 64 only in generic libraries, even though those dominate.
Not ideal for portable serialized formats.

types.definition: Unsigned integer with the same number of bits as the platform pointer type.
types.summary: Used pervasively for slice lengths, indexing, capacities, and many standard-library size APIs.
types.standardGuarantee: At least 16 bits wide; same width as pointer type.
specialRules: Best fit for lengths and indices, but not for portable on-disk/network numeric formats.
Unsigned subtraction can underflow; use checked/saturating patterns where appropriate.

mainstreamSizes.platform: all Rust targets with the type

types.rounding: Binary floating semantics; decimal fractions like 0.1 are usually inexact.
specialRules: NaN is not equal to itself.
Use total_cmp when a total ordering is needed for f32/f64.

mainstreamSizes.platform: all Rust targets with the type

types.rounding: Binary floating semantics; decimal fractions like 0.1 are usually inexact.
specialRules: NaN is not equal to itself.
Use total_cmp when a total ordering is needed for f32/f64.

types.definition: Rust char represents a Unicode scalar value, not a UTF-8 byte and not a UTF-16 code unit.
types.summary: A single Rust char is 4 bytes and can hold one Unicode scalar value, excluding surrogate code points.
types.standardGuarantee: Conceptually one Unicode scalar value; 4-byte storage on stable Rust.
specialRules: Different from C/C++ char and Java/Kotlin Char semantics.
A user-perceived grapheme cluster may require multiple chars.

types.definition: Boolean primitive with values true and false.
types.summary: Logical truth value type, not an integer substitute.
types.standardGuarantee: Exactly two values: true and false.
specialRules: Do not assume memory layout compatibility with C bool without explicit FFI contract.
